content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

{"change_summary":"Implemented the xrwl drain-loop harness remediation. The scratch drain harness now drives `drain_bead_queue` through the real production entry points for project creation (`create_project_from_bead`), PR open/gates/squash (`open_pr_for_completed_run`), and PR polling/known-flake rerun/merge (`watch_pr`), with lower-level in-memory mocks for br, feature branches, PR tooling, PR watch tooling, deterministic clock, run completion snapshots, bead close, and follow-up filing. The backend-exhausted scenario now includes a second ready happy-path bead and asserts skip-plus-continue behavior across two cycles. The happy-path test also asserts that the real PR-open path invoked gates, reset, commit, push, and PR creation through the mock `PrToolPort`. Existing scenario coverage remains in place for happy path, known flake rerun, permanent failure follow-up abort, backend-exhausted skip, interrupted resume, bot rejection abort, force-complete merge, and `RecoveryAction` exhaus

## Steps

1. [Completed] codex implementation turn

## Validation Evidence

None provided.

## Outstanding Risks

None identified.
